revision.text:
{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)}} * [http://www.npweb.com/kouzanji/ Official Site (Japanese)]
{{Buddhist temples in Japan}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kozan-ji Shimonoseki}} [[Category:1327 establishments in Asia]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Rinzai school]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:1320s establishments in Japan]]

This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)}} * [http://www.npweb.com/kouzanji/ Official Site (Japanese)]

It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)}} * [http://www.npweb.com/kouzanji/ Official Site (Japanese)]

It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)}} * [http://www.npweb.com/kouzanji/ Official Site (Japanese)]

It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.

It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)}} * [http://www.npweb.com/kouzanji/ Official Site (Japanese)]

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)}} * [http://www.npweb.com/kouzanji/ Official Site (Japanese)]
{{Buddhist temples in Japan}}

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)}} * [http://www.npweb.com/kouzanji/ Official Site (Japanese)]

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)}} * [http://www.npweb.com/kouzanji/ Official Site (Japanese)]

It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].

This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]

Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)}} * [http://www.npweb.com/kouzanji/ Official Site (Japanese)]

{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]

It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.

revision.text:
{{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[Zen|Zen faction]] in [[Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[Butsuden]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[Shoin]] *[[Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *[[National Treasures of Japan]] **[[List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)}} * [http://www.npweb.com/kouzanji/ Official Site (Japanese)]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كوزان جى (شيمونوسيكى, ياماجوتشى, اليابان)]][[sn:commons:Category:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[de:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[fr:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:功山寺]][[zh:功山寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|prefecture={{ill|Shimonoseki|WD=Q203308}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.976988|N|130.961739|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Thousand-Armed Thousand-Eyed Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q10904910}}|part_of={{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974}}; {{ill|Q11471184|WD=Q11471184}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1088510]] Wikidata name: Kōzan-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai]] | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[:en:Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of [[:en:Zen|Zen faction]] in [[:en:Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was first established as a temple of the [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai]] school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura era]].
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. This architecture in {{nihongo|[[:en:Zenshūyō|Zenshūyō]]|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The [[:en:Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] No.19.
== Building list == *[[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)]] - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *[[:en:Shoin|Shoin]] *[[:en:Hattō|Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.

{{ill|Q11471184|WD=Q11471184}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1088510]] Wikidata name: Kōzan-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai}} | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[:en:Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of {{ill|Zen|en|Zen|WD=Q7953|lt=Zen faction}} in [[:en:Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was first established as a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai}} school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura era}}.
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan}}. This architecture in {{nihongo|{{ill|Zenshūyō|en|Zenshūyō|WD=Q3575224|lt=Zenshūyō}}|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} No.19.
== Building list == *{{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|WD=Q17051413|lt=Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)}} - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *{{ill|Shoin|en|Shoin|WD=Q3482442|lt=Shoin}} *[[:en:Hattō|Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *{{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure (Japan)}} **{{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)|WD=Q3245523|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)}} * [http://www.npweb.com/kouzanji/ Official Site (Japanese)]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كوزان جى (شيمونوسيكى, ياماجوتشى, اليابان)]][[co:commons:Category:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[de:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[fr:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:功山寺]][[zh:功山寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كوزان جى (شيمونوسيكى, ياماجوتشى, اليابان)]][[co:commons:Category:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[de:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[fr:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:功山寺]][[zh:功山寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:كوزان جى (شيمونوسيكى, ياماجوتشى, اليابان)]][[sn:commons:Category:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[de:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[en:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[fr:Kōzan-ji (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:功山寺]][[zh:功山寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|prefecture={{ill|Shimonoseki|WD=Q203308}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.976988|N|130.961739|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Thousand-Armed Thousand-Eyed Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q10904910}}|part_of={{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974}}; {{ill|Q11471184|WD=Q11471184}}}} {{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai}} | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[:en:Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of {{ill|Zen|en|Zen|WD=Q7953|lt=Zen faction}} in [[:en:Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was first established as a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai}} school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura era}}.
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan}}. This architecture in {{nihongo|{{ill|Zenshūyō|en|Zenshūyō|WD=Q3575224|lt=Zenshūyō}}|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} No.19.
== Building list == *{{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|WD=Q17051413|lt=Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)}} - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *{{ill|Shoin|en|Shoin|WD=Q3482442|lt=Shoin}} *[[:en:Hattō|Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *{{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure (Japan)}} **{{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)|WD=Q3245523|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)}}

{{ill|Q11471184|WD=Q11471184|1=Q11471184}}}} {{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai|1=Rinzai school}} | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[:en:Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of {{ill|Zen|en|Zen|WD=Q7953|lt=Zen faction|1=Zen}} in [[:en:Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was first established as a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625|lt=Rinzai|1=Rinzai school}} school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura era|1=Kamakura period}}.
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}. This architecture in {{nihongo|{{ill|Zenshūyō|en|Zenshūyō|WD=Q3575224|lt=Zenshūyō|1=Zenshūyō}}|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q987974|lt=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} No.19.
== Building list == *{{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|WD=Q17051413|lt=Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|1=Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)}} - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *{{ill|Shoin|en|Shoin|WD=Q3482442|lt=Shoin|1=Shoin}} *[[:en:Hattō|Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
== See also == *{{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure (Japan)|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} **{{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)|WD=Q3245523|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (temples)}}

{{ill|Q11471184|qid=Q11471184|1=Q11471184}}}} {{Short description|Buddhist temple in Shimonoseki, Japan}}{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kinzan Kōzan-ji | native_name = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Kozanji Temple (Shimonoseki).JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image_size = | alt = | caption = Butsuden | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1-2-3, Kawabata, Chofu, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.976988|130.961739|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|qid=Q639625|lt=Rinzai|1=Rinzai school}} | deity = | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = https://web.archive.org/web/20091201030220/http://npweb.com/kouzanji/ | founded_by = Kyoan Genjaku | year_completed = 1327 }} [[File:141122 Kozanji Shimonoseki Yamaguchi pref Japan11n.jpg|thumb|Shoin Garden]] The {{nihongo|'''Kinzan Kōzan-ji'''|金山功山寺}} is a temple of the [[:en:Sōtō school|Sōtō]] school of {{ill|Zen|en|Zen|qid=Q7953|lt=Zen faction|1=Zen}} in [[:en:Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki]], {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|qid=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was first established as a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai school|en|Rinzai school|qid=Q639625|lt=Rinzai|1=Rinzai school}} school by [[Kyoan Genjaku]] in 1327 of the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura era|1=Kamakura period}}.
Kōzan-ji's Butsuden, completed in 1320, is a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|qid=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}. This architecture in {{nihongo|{{ill|Zenshūyō|en|Zenshūyō|qid=Q3575224|lt=Zenshūyō|1=Zenshūyō}}|[[:ja:禅宗様|禅宗様]]}} (aka Zen'yō) style (Zen style), combining Japanese and Chinese Buddhist design features. It is the oldest building designed in the Zenshūyō style that exists in Japan.
The {{ill|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qid=Q987974|lt=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Chūgoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} No.19.
== Building list == *{{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|qid=Q17051413|lt=Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|1=Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)}} - National Treasure of Japan. It was built in 1320. *[[Sanmon]] - It was rebuilt in 1773. *{{ill|Shoin|en|Shoin|qid=Q3482442|lt=Shoin|1=Shoin}} *[[:en:Hattō|Hattō]] *[[Kyōzō]] - It was rebuilt in 1799.
